mechanism_narrative: A persistently high but stabilizing FEDFUNDS rate signals that the tightening cycle is nearing its terminal level, reducing incremental discount-rate shock to equities and producing modestly positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as markets price a near-term pause.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.1% Dec-2022 vintage, near cycle peak
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level/trend and 20d forward returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-return hit rate in high-rate periods is not statistically above the unconditional 65.5% base rate (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window signals labor-market deterioration consistent with an emerging Sahm-rule-style recession signal, causing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days to be negative as growth expectations are revised down.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.5% Dec-2022, historically low, low revision risk
failure_condition: When trigger fires, 20d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the negative-return hit rate is not significantly different from the unconditional negative rate (34.1%) at p>0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds the trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL-implied inflation rate by a wide margin (deeply positive real policy rate) and CPIAUCSL is simultaneously decelerating month-over-month, the combination signals restrictive real policy bite without disinflation credit yet priced in, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as tightening risk dominates before any pivot narrative solidifies.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12m CPIAUCSL YoY change > 1.5pp AND CPIAUCSL MoM deceleration
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL Dec-2022 vintage 298.112 vs revised 298.832, decelerating YoY pace
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with decelerating inflation
failure_condition: Joint-trigger observations show 5d forward returns positive in more than 50% of cases, or Rank IC of the joint real-rate interaction signal is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During an active monetary tightening cycle, a sustained decline in HOUST (housing starts) reflects rate-sensitive demand contraction that leads broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; outside tightening regimes the same HOUST decline has muted or reversed predictive power.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines for 3+ consecutive months while FEDFUNDS is rising or at cycle highs
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage 1427.0 Nov-2022 revised to 1424.0, declining trend into tightening cycle
failure_condition: During tightening-cycle regimes with declining HOUST, 60d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect disappears (Rank IC < 0.03) when restricted to tightening-cycle subsample.

mechanism_narrative: A rising 6-month trend in INDPRO signals expanding industrial output and supply-side momentum that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as production strength feeds through to profit expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO Nov-2022 vintage 104.5175 revised down to 100.9552, moderate revision risk
failure_condition: Rank IC between 6-month INDPRO trend and 60d forward returns remains below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or positive hit rate during rising-INDPRO periods is not above base rate at p>0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly PAYEMS growth decelerates below its trailing 6-month average while UNRATE simultaneously ticks up, the joint signal captures an inflection in labor demand that historically precedes broader risk-off repricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; PAYEMS deceleration alone without UNRATE confirmation is insufficient.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM growth below trailing 6m average AND UNRATE up MoM
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS Dec-2022 vintage 153743 revised to 154342, high revision risk noted
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances show 20d forward returns positive in more than 55% of cases, or interaction signal Rank IC is not meaningfully higher than either single-series signal's IC over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in RSAFS exceeding 0.5% signals an abrupt contraction in consumer spending that raises near-term recession probability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets quickly reprice consumption-driven earnings risk.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS Nov-2022 vintage 689443 revised down to 659458, medium revision risk
failure_condition: 5d forward returns following RSAFS decline trigger are positive in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC over any rolling 2-year window falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a sustained deceleration in CPIAUCSL YoY inflation signals approaching policy pivot potential, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as markets price in a lower terminal rate; the same disinflation trend in a non-tightening regime carries no comparable pivot premium.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL YoY decelerating into Dec-2022 while FEDFUNDS near cycle high of 4.1%
failure_condition: Restricted to tightening-cycle subsample, 60d forward returns during disinflation trends are not significantly more positive than the unconditional base rate (p>0.05), or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.
